programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. programme.desc: An addict holds Luke at gunpoint, demanding he hand over the key to the drugs box, but a struggle breaks out and one of them is fatally shot. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June's retirement party is put on hold when she and her ex-husband are taken hostage by the drugs gang. programme.desc: The meeting with his solicitor spells disaster for Nigel and Grant makes a promise he probably won't be able to keep

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Tom helps a swimming instructor come to terms with a serious bowel condition which requires him to be fitted with a colostomy bag, and a meals-on-wheels driver recently passed fit by Will suffers a stroke at the wheel and nearly crashes into Kate, but refuses to be taken into a nursing home to aid his recovery. Medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Gray O'Brien and Maggie O'Neill

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James is dreading the staging of the Darrowby Show's dog event because the temperamental Tricky Woo is bound to be a competitor. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean and Lionel settle down to enjoy a quiet evening, only to find themselves constantly disturbed by ominous noises from the empty house next door. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard has an accident and Hyacinth decides to buy a small country retreat - but her plans go awry in a most unexpected fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth learns the Barker-Finches had a local celebrity at their barbecue, and is desperate to outdo them with her own event - an 'outdoors indoors luxury barbecue and finger buffet'. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Roy Barraclough guest stars as Crowcroft, a well-known Lothario with a preference for gullible women. When he samples Nora Batty's baking, he decides to make her his next target. Long-running comedy, with Kathy Staff, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: The Da Vinci Code (2006)
programme.desc: A respected academic goes on the run when he is framed for the murder of a French historian. Pursued by the detective on the case and a sinister assassin monk, he strives to unmask the culprit, leading to a conspiracy determined to preserve a 2,000-year-old secret at any cost. Thriller based on Dan Brown's novel, starring Tom Hanks, Audrey Tautou, Ian McKellen, Paul Bettany and Jean Reno

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Halford visits a clairvoyant in the hope of making contact with his dead wife, but instead receives a message from a teenage girl who was abducted and left to die in a transport container in 1982. Despite the unconventional nature of the new evidence, Pullman agrees to reopen the investigation. The ageing detectives become convinced it will be their final case when they are ordered to report for physical and psychological evaluation. James Bolam, Amanda Redman and Robert Bathurst star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A teenager holds the key to an assassination plot having witnessed a murder. Harry is contacted by a Russian sleeper agent with a file containing information about the traitor in his team, but he is unprepared for the shocking revelations

Medical drama, starring Maggie O'Neill and Simon Shepherd, with a guest appearance by Frank Windsor

programme.desc: James is impressed when he visits Granville Bennett's animal hospital with Siegfried, and is interested to learn that it is looking for a new partner. James Grout (Inspector Morse) guest stars, with Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean begins to think that, 19 months after tying the knot, her marriage may have become stale. She wonders whether a few surprises from Lionel would spice things up, so Alistair, helped by Judith and Sandy, comes up with a plan to give her the treat of a lifetime

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth agrees to meet the Ladies' Luncheon Club's VIP at the railway station - but the assignment proves more difficult than imagined. Nigel Davenport guest stars

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth continues her search for a small country retreat - but her idea of small is not quite the same as everybody else's. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg's decision to write his memoirs causes Howard a great deal of concern, while Smiler is devastated when a blossoming relationship ends suddenly. Long-running comedy series, starring Peter Sallis, Robert Fyfe and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley and Havers investigate when young lawyer Emily Proctor is found axed to death in Hyde Park. They are led to the chambers of a human rights barrister who was having an affair with her, and it isn't long before the victim's seedy double life involving Internet porn and dark family secrets emerges. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Samuel West

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad is ordered by its new boss to look into the unsolved murder of a barrister found bound and gagged in his car 25 years ago. The case was a rare failure for a former chief inspector, although his notes point to a very clear suspect. As they uncover the late advocate's unusual hobbies, they gradually learn exactly what happened. Anita Dobson, Jenny Agutter and Keith Barron guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry is arrested after being framed as the mole who revealed classified information, and the head of MI5 orders his interrogation. With Britain under threat of nuclear attack from Russia, the whole team comes under suspicion and all communication is monitored. After a trusted contact is murdered, Lucas is horrified when he realises the identity of the double agent. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: An overworked Smithy loses his cool and accidentally reveals to a worried woman that her husband has been held on suspicion of stalking a prostitute. Mickey undertakes PR work with the local Neighbourhood Watch group on the Jasmine Allen Estate, but when he makes two arrests during a drugs deal, he becomes drawn into investigating a joyriding incident. Michael Maloney guest stars

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives investigate the death of a woman who has been killed in a hit-and-run incident. Suspicion falls on the man who found her body, but he denies ever having met her. Lynley and Havers visit the victim's estranged husband and son, neither of whom has seen her since she walked out after her daughter's murder 30 years previously. Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small star

programme.desc: Maddy puts her career at risk when she agrees to secretly treat a stabbing victim as a favour for her sister, who has recently been released from prison. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harassed Harry is forced to close the department to members of the public after a prison riot leaves several inmates injured, and the undue stress he's under causes him to lash out at Lara, blaming her for Tally's predicament. During the melee in A&E, Roxy is taken hostage, while a charming journalist cons Abs into letting her have more access than is allowed. TV presenter Josie D'Arby guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy encounters Ryan Jones again and finds the battered corpse of his wife Connie in the boot of his car. As the murderer makes his getaway in the London Underground, it soon becomes clear he has a personal vendetta against the Sun Hill desk sergeant and plans further killings, working his way through Smithy's address book - beginning with his mother Pauline. Michael Maloney guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline decides to go to see Michelle in the USA. Tiffany stirs up Bianca making her realise Ricky is lying about Sam's divorce

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca puts Ricky on the spot about a divorce. Nigel and Liam confront each other at Clare's custody hearing. Felix opens his new hairdressers

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Regina and George return to Ash Park from their wedding, and are warned about their marriage of convenience by Elizabeth and Carolyn. Meanwhile, Sarah attempts to arrange Rene's funeral and is offered emotional and practical support by Roy and Doris Collins. Elsewhere, Gino accidentally finds and reads Ann's novel, and Elizabeth is invited to a function in Sydney by Douglas Goddard

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will's career is thrown into turmoil when one of his patients has an epileptic fit while driving and is killed in the ensuing crash. Meanwhile, Tom is puzzled by Alex's cold attitude towards him and Carol struggles as her loyalties are put to the test. Medical drama, starring Gray O'Brien, Maggie O'Neill and Simon Shepherd

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A London banker who is an old school friend of James comes to stay for a long weekend, and gives him advice about going into partnership with Granville Bennett. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean calls on Alistair's help to do a spot of match-making and Lionel finds himself in the middle of a muddle. Comedy, with Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard finally move into their new country retreat, and while she thinks it is 'olde worlde bijou', he finds it a decided disappointment. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth press-gangs a reluctant Richard into helping repair the lights at the local church hall. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler begins attending a poetry group and is inspired to write verses for an enchanted Marina. When the two go to the meeting together, an anxious Howard needs to find a way of keeping an eye on them without being seen - and Truly claims to have the perfect solution

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved-crime squad reopens the case of an Asian woman who was attacked eight years ago, and has been in a coma ever since. After enlisting the aid of a community liaison officer, it becomes apparent there is conflict in the victim's family, as well as a possible connection with the disappearance of a private detective. Nina Wadia, Keith Allen and Navin Chowdhry guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Russian sleeper agents plan to detonate a nuclear bomb in London, leaving the team with no alternative but to kidnap the mole and force her to help prevent the attack. Under interrogation she reveals that vital information is contained in a dossier hidden at London Bridge - but as Section D members race to retrieve it, they are met by a hail of gunfire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy encounters Ryan Jones again and finds the battered corpse of his wife Connie in the boot of his car. As the murderer makes his getaway in the London Underground, it soon becomes clear he has a personal vendetta against the Sun Hill desk sergeant and plans further killings, working his way through Smithy's address book - beginning with his mother Pauline. programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph restores a trolley at Earlswood Recycling Centre, while Chloe Kempster overhauls a vintage table and chest and Duncan McKean transforms an antique floor lamp

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a fairground water can, a camel saddle and a RAF mascot teddy bear, restored as a tribute to the airman it belonged to

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian gleefully warns Max he now has the upper hand on him - unless Max wants the whole Square to know who he has been kissing. Tina visits Mick, who is despondent after his day at the care home, but she assumes he is upset because of losing the Vic and sets out to get rid of Ian for good. Meanwhile, Jack and Whitney warn Tiffany about her surrogacy plans and Thompson confronts Callum

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha makes a controversial decision while operating on a cancerous patient, which leaves Max, Donna and Dom all concerned about his actions and general welfare - but their interference results in him taking out his frustrations on Jodie. Cameron tries to get back on a furious Chloe's good side, but his actions have disastrous consequences for a patient. Clayton demands that Kian come clean about his drug habit

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton reports on the businesses fighting to survive the pandemic as customers are left unable to make use of their services, from the adventure playground with no children to an events company with no events. Filmed over the course of the first and second lockdowns, this programme reveals the pressure on those trying to save jobs and livelihoods.

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four chefs competing in this round are challenged to prepare either potato rosti served with egg, bacon and tomatoes or tempura mussels with a smoked garlic aioli for their skills test. For the second round, they have one hour and 25 minutes to cook their own two-course signature dishes and secure themselves a place in the quarter finals

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Fraser and Caitlin become close, but even as they grow to understand each other, they remain unsure about their own sexual identities. Their friendship creates a safe space in which Caitlin starts to consider the idea of a sex change, while Fraser develops a crush on a soldier in his mother's chain of command

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Soldier Craig is about to be deployed on a mission in Afghanistan. The feeling of impending danger and the imminent departure of a key member of their group push the kids to embrace the present with unrestrained urgency, as they organise an impromptu send-off party at an abandoned villa

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore a 1930s semi in Hatfield in Hertfordshire, a flat in Croydon, south London and a workshop with land in Heanor, Derbyshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A prisoner claiming for injury behind bars forgets that the camera never lies and a bodybuilder is floored after flaunting his workouts when he is supposed to be off sick

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: A farmer works the night shift as two of his cows are expecting. Meanwhile, a locksmith and a plumber are called out after dark to two very different late night emergencies

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Tim Weeks and Colin Young help the red and blue teams find bargains in the antique shops of York, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Clifton Park Museum in Rotherham

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Following the death of his father, a vulnerable man finds himself targeted, and Karen works to find out who may be after him. Meanwhile, Emma opens up to Jimmi

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple wanting to relocate from the coast to the Herefordshire countryside on a budget of £330,000. The presenter also finds out about a mobile cider press

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items from Walsall Recycling Centre in the West Midlands, restoring and repairing retro tulip chairs, some old fabric and a bureau

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, including a rare bicycle side car, a Victorian sewing stand and military drum

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. The first arrival of the day is widow Eve Robinson from Kings Lynn, Norfolk, who hopes that goldsmith Richard Talman can mend her diamond wedding ring. Next, Wizard Geoff turns his attention to a vintage penny arcade game which was salvaged from a bonfire over 40 years ago. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a shop mannequin and a pair of lumberjack's tree spurs

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. First, Monica asks two chefs to fillet and cook john dory and serve with a vibrant green parsley sauce and a garlic cream, before the second pair of competitors make poached figs with honeycomb and a flavoured cream for Marcus. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan set the artists their final tasks as they face a panel of social media influencers, before performing on a BBC Radio 1Xtra show

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Sam is determined to fight in a public match. Imogen needs to turn bad, and James attempts his most daring leap yet

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former swimmer Fran Halsall, ex-Everton footballer Leon Osman, three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby and Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of an opportunist attempting to exaggerate his personal injury claim, and insurers deal with a rear-end shunt that has plenty of twists and turns

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: The volunteers from Northumbria Blood Bikes who transfer life-saving supplies between hospitals, and the Northern Powergrid team tackling power cuts through the night

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of medieval church paintings

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al fails to put the brakes on his relationship with Lily, Karen's good deed backfires and Sid is called to treat a stab wound - but the teenage victim refuses to reveal the culprit

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £375,000 to play with, Ginny Buckley joins in the search for a rural home in north Devon with adjoining land for animals. During her time in the county, Ginny joins the harvest at a traditional Devonshire orchard where over 70 of the country's heritage apple varieties are grown

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore rescues a vintage pram in the hope of generating a profit for its owners. A windbreaker and an old ottoman are also spruced up

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a satchel owned by a prisoner of war, a pub clock with an intriguing past and a sea-serpent pen holder. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby prepares to announce who has won the Lucy Beale Award, but he still doesn't know who to give the money to, so Ian tells him he knows who Lucy would have wanted to win. Tina and Shirley are eager to see Ian's downfall, Phil and Sharon's divorce gets ugly and a furious Ben will stop at nothing to find out who informed on the Mitchells

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The strongest chefs compete in the last quarter-final, where they are challenged to create a dish that captures the essence of the forest, making the most of ingredients from meat to foraging produce such as mushrooms and berries. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Grace Dent and Jay Rayner, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anti-fraud teams deal with a crash-for-cash duo when an £8 million scam is exposed, and food poisoning leaves one insurer with a claim for eight holiday-makers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, then she and two experts transform a sideboard, a cane chair and cider demi-johns

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets people working in the gas industry in and around the Cumbrian coast. She travels by helicopter to the Morecambe gas field as engineers find new ways of extracting the remaining fuel supply that has been exploited for more than 35 years. Keeley also heads for the Barrow gas terminal where a complex network of pipes sends the gas to local homes

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the latest last-32 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Then world champion Judd Trump suffered a shock defeat at this stage last year when he was beaten 6-3 by veteran Nigel Bond. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt are in Glemham Hall, Suffolk, where they uncover a Victorian peddler doll complete with wicker basket, while Paul Martin explores Framlingham Castle

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: An interview with Jamie Laing and Karen Hauer, who tangoed their way into the quarter-final after winning the dance-off on Sunday night. Plus, backstage gossip and training updates

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Olympic gold medal-winning swimmer Rebecca Adlington and TV presenter Helen Skelton rummage for items of interest - and value - in the north west of England, assisted by antique experts Roo Irvine and Tim Medhurst. En route to auction in Bolton, they discover the salty secret of Cheshire cheese and hear the story of how, thanks to a Hawaiian Duke, swimming was changed for ever

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver oversees proceedings as previous winners of the culinary contest prepare a feast for the heroes of the pandemic - beginning with a course of canapes. In a twist on previous series, the eight cooks will be split into two teams of four and will judge the other group's efforts in a blind taste test. Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen will be joining the teams in the kitchen

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: Highlights compiled from recent series of QI, the comedy quiz hosted by Sandi Toksvig

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Robert throws a narcotic-fuelled gathering for Harper's birthday and the pair stay up all night in a haze of drink and drugs. Harper heads into work the next day and is horrified to learn she has made a trading error that will cost the desk £50,000, and desperately attempts to fix her mistake in fear of losing her job

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe introduces a special festive edition of the comedy show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Chris McCausland and musical comedy double act Flo & Joan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the day's last-32 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents extended highlights of one of the day's best last-32 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: With sky high property prices, home ownership seems out of reach to many. The government's shared ownership scheme is supposed to help get people on the property ladder by letting them buy part of their home and rent the rest, but this programme hears from shared owners who say uncapped service charges, building defects and costly lease extensions have left them stuck in homes with escalating costs and huge debts

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The presenter explores some of the myths swirling around the French Revolution of 1789 - an uprising that brought down the nation's royal family. This violent revolution became the blueprint of many future revolutions across the world, but what exactly happened during this turbulent period is open to manipulation and interpretation. Historian Michael Rapport explains how the revolution was not started by starving peasants as many assume but was in fact sparked by a group of lawyers and property owners

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs must French trim a rack of Lamb and cook a portion served with a mint pesto, with the second pair of competitors asked to fillet a plaice and cook one portion, and serve with a chicken butter sauce and pickled radish. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.desc: Christina Trevanion trawls the programme's archives and picks out some of the best Victorian antiques bought by the reds and blue teams over the years. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Southwell Racecourse with a mysterious Victorian glass object and Charlie Ross finds out just how seriously the Victorians took crime and punishment on a visit to Anglesey

programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph restores a trolley at Earlswood Recycling Centre, while Chloe Kempster overhauls a vintage table and chest and Duncan McKean transforms an antique floor lamp

programme.desc: Observational documentary following the reality of life in Britain's biggest special school. In the first episode pupils face their fears at an outdoor adventure centre. The focus of the trip is for the young people to have an opportunity to challenge their disability physically, and abseiling in a wheelchair epitomises this experience

programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening last-16 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.desc: TV presenting duo Sam Nixon and Mark Rhodes join experts Charles Hanson and Natasha Raskin Sharp on the hunt for bargains to sell at auction. Charles and Mark discover one of their items is not all it seems, while Sam and Natasha find a clock with dual nationality before the contest is settled at an auction in Darlington, Co Durham

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the canape and starter heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. With Andi Oliver hosting proceedings, there is a vacancy in the judging chamber, so joining Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton is actor and comedian Kerry Godliman

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Elizabeth is distraught when her debtors finally catch up with her. Meanwhile, William decides that he must gain support if he is to rescue Jack from the slavers, and Kate finally catches her prince. Starring Lesley Manville, Liv Tyler and Pippa Bennet-Warner

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Alan Davies are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Susan Calman and Matt Lucas for this extended edition of the long-running panel show - and this week it's a festive special, so Sandi's elves have been busy coming up with tough questions on the subject of - what else? - Noel. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers considered to be most interesting

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Volunteers in Hastings rush to the aid of a surfer, who had sought to take advantage of the huge waves created by a storm at sea, but has now ended up going missing. On the north coast of Cornwall, the crew of St Agnes race to reach three people caught in a treacherous rip current that is pulling them out to sea, while in Scotland, the crew of Invergordon lifeboat is called out when two men are spotted clinging to an oil rig's anchor chain

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists are challenged to create a dish inspired by seaside, using a choice of fish, shellfish and vegetables at their disposal. But as always, Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing expect to see refined food with an imaginative twist. And it's not over yet, as they then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Grace Dent and Tom Parker Bowles, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, the judges will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian house in London's Crystal Palace, a chalet bungalow in Rackheath near Norwich, and a terrace house in Canterbury

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items from Walsall Recycling Centre in the West Midlands, restoring and repairing retro tulip chairs, some old fabric and a bureau

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the latest last-16 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Ding Junhui edged out seven-time champion Ronnie O'Sullivan 6-5 as he continued his march to the title at this stage last year. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visits the University of Nottingham, where antique expert Caroline Hawley is wowed by a huge Shakespearean-themed quilt and Charles Hanson comes across an 18th-century handwritten Valentine that is hotly contested at the auction. Presenter Paul Martin visits a gorge in north Nottinghamshire called Creswell Crags, where in 2003 prehistoric cave art was discovered

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Weekday fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, featuring interviews with the couples and a sneak peek at what they will be wearing on Saturday with designer Vicky Gill

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the concluding last-16 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. At this stage last year, Liang Wenbo prevailed 6-4 in an all-Chinese match, and John Higgins won by the same scoreline against fellow former world champion Stuart Bingham. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Woody the St Bernard moves in with five-year-old Dexter, who has cerebral palsy. Dexter's family, in Surrey, are hoping that the little pup will become his companion and learn to carry out simple assistance tasks. In Hull, Mungo joins Alastair, Daron and their possessive older cocker spaniel Milo - but the dogs clash from day one, and Alastair and Daron call on Louise to help

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: Documentary following the rigid, class-obsessed Britain of the 1950s to the more liberal, inclusive society in the latter part of the Queen's reign. Andrew Marr begins by taking in the liberal victories of the 'permissive society' - as well as the ferocious backlash of middle England at the perceived erosion of family values. He also looks at people's attitudes to movie starlet Diana Dors and wannabe movie starlet Ruth Ellis - who achieved fame of a completely different kind

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne and Rhys James. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that happened in the week the programme first aired in June 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the day's last-16 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents extended highlights of one of the day's best last-16 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the fourth episode, Angela Scanlon, Nigel Harman and Rachel Parris take a light-hearted look back at the year 2003, from Beyonce's hit Crazy in Love, to David Blaine living in a box over the Thames

programme.title: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Fall of an Icon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the Myanmar leader's fall from grace. While under house arrest during the country's military dictatorship, she became a Nobel prize-winning champion of democracy, human rights and peaceful protest. However, since becoming the leader of her nation, she has been implicated in brutal atrocities against the Rohingya Muslims, and faced allegations of genocide at the International Court of Justice. Key figures in international politics discuss the reasons why the qualities that brought her such acclaim have been in marked contrast to the realities of her leadership

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: Fred looks at early experiments in the use of steam for road transport, more than 100 years before the invention of the petrol engine. Plus, an insight into the development of the traction engine for agriculture and haulage

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture, creating a mountain scene with soft, sloping grass cover, a crystal lake and rocky banks

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian continues his personal review of the 1980s, considering how by the middle of the decade, Britain was wrestling with two contradictory impulses - the rise of a strong nationalist sentiment and the emergence of an increasingly globalised world. Along the way Sandbrook examines everything from the invasion of the Falkland Islands to the invasion of the home computer. He examines the moral panic surrounding video nasties, looks at the Americanisation of UK culture and the picket-line skirmishes playing nightly on TV, as well as the Aids epidemic and the threat of the IRA

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher's struggling public image was transformed in 1982 when Argentine forces occupied the British overseas territory of the Falkland Islands in the South Atlantic, with the triumph of the British forces making her reputation in the UK and around the world. On the home front she faced another challenge to her leadership from National Union of Miners, which called a UK-wide strike, while in 1984 the IRA bombed the Grand Hotel in Brighten in an attempt to kill her and her most senior colleagues

programme.title: The Falklands Play
programme.desc: Production of Ian Curteis's controversial drama which charts the political manoeuvring that went on behind the scenes of Margaret Thatcher's government during the Falklands War. Patricia Hodge stars as the prime minister, with James Fox and John Standing. First aired in 2002

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found murdered at the abandoned boys' home, and her body has similar wounds to those found in the previous two killings. Kata and Arnar are under pressure to identify and capture the killer. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata, Arnar and the Reykjavik team examine boxes of documents from an inquiry into Icelandic children's homes in an effort to identify both the next possible victim and the killer. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture, creating a mountain scene with soft, sloping grass cover, a crystal lake and rocky banks

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: Fred looks at early experiments in the use of steam for road transport, more than 100 years before the invention of the petrol engine. Plus, an insight into the development of the traction engine for agriculture and haulage

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher's struggling public image was transformed in 1982 when Argentine forces occupied the British overseas territory of the Falkland Islands in the South Atlantic, with the triumph of the British forces making her reputation in the UK and around the world. On the home front she faced another challenge to her leadership from National Union of Miners, which called a UK-wide strike, while in 1984 the IRA bombed the Grand Hotel in Brighten in an attempt to kill her and her most senior colleagues

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: The development of the steamship, from the earliest paddle boats and the first iron-hulled vessels to modern sport screw propellers and ships powered by steam turbines, such as the Royal Yacht Britannia

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene in which he shows how the exciting colours of autumn can be captured on canvas

programme.title: Winterwatch 1963 - The Big Freeze
programme.desc: Chris Packham introduces a documentary from the BBC's archive that takes a look at the worst British winter of the 20th century. He also explores how this severe weather 50 years ago affected the nation's wildlife and how it would cope if another equally bad winter occurred

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls explores the extraordinary ecosystem that covers an area larger than Great Britain, revealing that only seven per cent of it is coral reef. The rest is a variety of interconnected habitats, including hundreds of islands, mangrove swamps, deep-water gardens, vast sand flats, meadows of sea grass and what is said to be the world's oldest jungle. All are interconnected by a giant deep-water lagoon - and all are full of amazing wildlife

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Film critic Catherine Bray hosts a celebration of cinematic guilty pleasures, from Showgirls and Love Actually, to Cats and The Room

programme.title: Some People with Jokes
programme.desc: A selection of people from around Britain are joined by their beloved pooches while they share favourite gags with the viewing public, with everything from one-liners to shaggy dog stories proving it's not just their four-legged friends that are barking

programme.title: Make! Craft Britain
programme.desc: A programme celebrating all things handmade, following ordinary people as they learn new skills taught and take part in craft workshops led by inspirational teachers. In the first episode, two groups of novice crafters master the art of hooky rugmaking and traditional letterpress. Elsewhere, origami artist Sam Tsang demonstrates how to make something beautiful using a single sheet of paper

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the changing fortunes of a family of architects, following the rise and fall of George Gilbert Scott, his son George Junior, and grandson Giles. Presenter Dan Cruickshank finds out how the dynasty was responsible for the creation of iconic landmarks such as the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station, and helped shape the Victorian age with their designs

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene in which he shows how the exciting colours of autumn can be captured on canvas

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls explores the extraordinary ecosystem that covers an area larger than Great Britain, revealing that only seven per cent of it is coral reef. The rest is a variety of interconnected habitats, including hundreds of islands, mangrove swamps, deep-water gardens, vast sand flats, meadows of sea grass and what is said to be the world's oldest jungle. programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: An examination of the major advances made with the invention of the steam turbine, which is still used today to generate electricity in coal-fired and nuclear power stations. programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an image of crashing waves in the moonlight, and demonstrates what can be achieved when painting on to a black canvas

programme.title: High Society (1956)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the artist over the course of the last year to chart her creative process at work, and talks about her life to date and how she sees the rest of her career. Tracey discusses her troubled early years in Margate and the series of breakthroughs in the 1990s that saw her become a leading light of the Young British Artists, featuring career-defining work like My Bed and her embroidered tent Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995. Contributors include Sir Nicholas Serota, Jay Jopling, Maria Balshaw and David Dawson

programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.desc: A cowboy rides into Alphaland. Fun and games with the 26 letters that come to life, tell stories and make words

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Robert tells Justin his Giggle Gasket has broken, meaning he cannot laugh, so Justin does everything he can to help his best friend get his laughter back

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wakes up in a grumpy mood, and it falls to Mo to put a smile on his face. This proves more difficult than she imagines as every time she gets him close to smiling, he slips back into the grumps again. How is Mo going to cheer Tee up and get him to eat breakfast?

programme.title: Tiny Wonders
programme.desc: The Noggle is landing, there must be a Tiny Wonder to explore. The Nogglins spend a day at the seaside. There’s a shape surprise for all the Nogglins, but will Nono find his?

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny gets stuck in Padget's shop while playing a game of hide-and-seek with Flop

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The fruit tea-loving bird-like characters watch a man load a lorry with parcels and study the concept of things being 'underneath' other things. Narrated by Linda Bassett

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes to the theatre with his friends to watch a sensory performance. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble puts on a magic show for Aunt Polly and Lord Tumble

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po finds a teddy bear in Teletubbyland. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po excitedly show the teddy to the Tiddlytubbies and they all love it very much

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Roly is digging in the garden when his spade suddenly hits a key. Duggee has his key badge, so they can find out through trial and error what it opens. The squirrels go on an adventure to work out what the key opens and what could be inside

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: The monsters care for the school pet, Ringo the beetle.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid dresses up as a mountain climber and tries to make it to the top of Everest

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a special autumn walk that starts in the daytime and finishes in the dark, where they all get to drink hot chocolate. On the way, they discover where a beaver has been at work, play inside a tree, make shadows with their lanterns and even fly like bats!

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy sees a picture of a pineapple and gets the taste in his mouth that won't go away. Soon everything and everyone in the Art World looks and tastes like pineapple too

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a theatre of dancing washing machines and a memory puzzle of a warthog, and watch as Book Boy makes a picnic table.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: A clown frightens Tig, so Woolly explains to her he is just a man with a painted face

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Catfish is a popular fish in Thailand and today Pearlina is serving it with sticky rice

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb's friends make a cake for his birthday, but he gets carried away when sneakily tasting it, and leaves only a pile of crumbs

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: It is Halloween and the feisty girl dresses up as a pirate and to go trick-or-treating, but it is a stormy night and when she begins to feel unwell she has to take refuge at Grannie Island's house

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum has a surprise for Melody when they enjoy a day out at the seaside, and they have a go on the carousel

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Laa-Laa and Po pick delicious green beans. The Noo-noo puts the beans into the Tubby Custard Machine and out comes tasty, green Tubby Custard

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales

programme.desc: Grandad pays a visit to Josh, Zac and Nathan. The brothers prepare for his arrival by drawing pictures of some scary looking sea monsters

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels find Duggee cleaning and repairing his collection of antiques

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: After accidentally sending a slumbering Mr Tolly floating off to Owl Island, Peter immediately launches a rescue with his friends

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies’ very own motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that help make roads! Grace helps operate a road planer, checks out a road printing machine and then has a go in a road paver!

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to the Caribbean in search of some seagrass he needs for a rescued manatee.

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Naughty fairies Zipsy and Popsy put a spell on Princess Purl so that she sleeps for a gazillion years. The gang tries everything to wake her up but nothing works, not even a kiss from brave Sir Bevel. Can Bitz invent a way to save the day before Zipsy and Popsy put a spell on them too?

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Professor Muddles's new invention, the Quiz Fit, causes chaos for Keith as he tries to exercise and quiz at the same time. Meanwhile, Nana catches cabbages in her Panto Showdown

programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran see Cynthia building a new greenhouse and decide to build a den. But when the den is too small both of them to fit in, Gran Gran helps JoJo with a plan they can follow to build a bigger den, one that will fit Gran Gran and Panda inside too!

A young boy with leukaemia has a scare when his temperature plummets, but his sparky personality has the nurses smiling and joining in a dancing contest

programme.title: The Firing Line
programme.desc: 2020 has been heavily defined by the Covid-19 pandemic, which has led to social disruption around the world and the deepest economic recession since the Great Depression. The year has also seen a record number of entries for the Rory Peck Awards. The awards pay tribute to an international field of freelance journalists around the world who have continued to capture some of the most dramatic and important news and current affairs stories - working in difficult and dangerous circumstances. The Firing Line, presented by Yalda Hakim, features the finalists and the winners of the awards.

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: To celebrate BBC 100 Women, Click profiles the most inspiring and influential women in technology, from the leaders shaping our future to those finding solutions to the challenges thrown up by the pandemic. The women featured are those driving change, not just for women, but for all parts of society. Hearing their inspirational stories, we learn not only how these women are making an impact, but also the challenges they've faced and the diverse backgrounds they come from. Using in-depth profiles, we provide audiences with a rare and intimate glimpse into the lives of the women who are transforming the world of technology at a unique time in our history. Women who are steering global technology policy, fighting bias in artificial intelligence, using data from sewers to combat Covid-19 and creating fertility solutions. Others have founded companies focusing on mental health, online abuse, women's health, vertical farming and current transport restrictions. With rare-access only Click can

programme.title: Witness History
programme.desc: For this special, women-focused edition of Witness History, the BBC's Ciru Muriuki in Nairobi brings us five moments from the recent past as told to us by the women who were there. We hear from the daughter of the world's first female prime minister. We also learn about the fight for equal rights for women at the Western Wall in Jerusalem and the campaign for the right to take the contraceptive pill in Japan. Plus, Nasa's female space pioneers and one of the crew members of the Discovery Shuttle mission that put into orbit one of the most remarkable scientific instruments of all time, the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: The Firing Line
programme.desc: 2020 has been heavily defined by the Covid-19 pandemic, which has led to social disruption around the world and the deepest economic recession since the Great Depression. The year has also seen a record number of entries for the Rory Peck Awards. The awards pay tribute to an international field of freelance journalists around the world who have continued to capture some of the most dramatic and important news and current affairs stories - working in difficult and dangerous circumstances. The Firing Line, presented by Yalda Hakim, features the finalists and the winners of the awards.

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. He chooses his favourite pair, Daniel, run by his old mate Daniel Boulud, and Le Bernadine, where Eric Ripart is in charge. At the end of the day, James cooks back on the pier as sunset approaches and he reminisces on his journey of more than 3,000 miles

programme.desc: Klaus is diagnosed with fish cough, which is has a 99 per cent mortality rate. There is a life-saving surgery available, but it's too expensive and Stan refuses to pay for it. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paul Nicholas guest stars as a Swedish sailor who waits for his ship home in Bernie's funeral parlour - but may not be telling the truth about the contents of the coffin he's escorting. A trio of young joyriders steal Carol's car while she is attending to a patient and decide to experiment with the drugs they find in her medical bag as well as selling them on to interested parties. Rosie and Fergus struggle to get their fledgling relationship off the ground as a series of interruptions plague their dates, while Bellamy starts to wonder whether trying again with Gina is really such a good idea. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a guest at the Aidensfield Arms explains she's searching for her missing son, Blaketon offers to help - but as his hunt progresses, it becomes clear somebody is trying to do him harm. Peggy turns down David's offer of dinner on her birthday in favour of a date with an old flame, and Bellamy finally opens up about his troubles under pressure from Walker

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy is late for work after struggling with indigestion all night. Dee tells Will to stop pestering her. Zoe is shocked when Chris tells her about Kelly being pregnant

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah acts coldly towards Jack. Tony is adamant that Emma cannot keep her horse. Mandy does not want to take up Mrs Taylor's offer of selling underwear

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken struggles to compose a tribute to Derek for the funeral. Tricia considers moving in with Ray. Andy visits Steve in prison to apologise for getting his sentence increased

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa tries to help one of her pupils, a bright 10-year-old who finds it hard to fit in and responds to stress by running away from home. Mark seeks advice on how to remedy his lack of success with the opposite sex, while Martin encounters a delusional park ranger with an unusual imaginary friend - a giant squirrel called Anthony. Medical comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the death of a pensioner found stabbed on a train at the height of rush hour. The case has a huge impact on Joe's home life when his daughter is named as a key witness, and he struggles to maintain a professional distance. The trail leads the team to a quiet Northumberland coastal town where clues appear to lead to one particular street - but why are the residents so reluctant to talk? Starring Brenda Blethyn, with David Leon, Sonya Cassidy, Jon Morrison and Eva Birthistle

programme.desc: When a woman is found dead laid out in the shape of a crucifix on Jesus Green, Will and Geordie are led to a nearby convent where they meet steely Sister Grace, who rules over her flock with an iron fist. Even more sinister is the picture that starts to emerge as they match the nuns to a series of unsolved missing persons cases from the previous five years. Meanwhile, when Leonard's father, Russell, arrives in Grantchester, it's clear there's no love lost between parent and son. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Tracy-Ann Oberman and Sean Gilder star

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is terrified when Don kidnaps her. Vera gives Tricia a hard time about moving out. Chris hopes that Angie will sleep with him. Jim tells Andy not to throw his life away

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: One of Joan's old flames comes back into her life and proceeds to woo her all over again, but Martin discovers the man in question has a life-threatening heart condition, presenting him with a difficult decision. The irascible doctor's problems do not stop there, as a schoolgirl develops a crush on him, leading to a confrontation with her irate father. Meanwhile, romance is also in the air for Al and Elaine. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes, with a guest appearance by John Alderton

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called in when David Kenworthy, the son of a prominent local family, is found murdered on the beach at Whitley Bay. A witness spotted arcade owner Larry Crowe arguing with David on the night of the killing, and a search of the suspect's property uncovers a bloodied baseball bat. However, Larry has a cast-iron alibi and it's clear he wasn't the only person the victim met that night. A second death then leaves Vera facing mounting pressure to crack the case, and struggling to answer the question of why David was drawn to the seafront on the evening of his father's retirement. Brenda Blethyn stars in the murder mystery, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Life on board with the 1,400 crew who live and work for up to nine months a year on luxury cruise ship the Regal Princess. As an 11-day voyage from Copenhagen to St Petersburg gets under way, some of the guests are vying for the award of most travelled passenger, while new dancer Dulcie is hoping to make the grade as part of the entertainment team. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Ed Perrin has been given permission to sail his vessel all the way into Stockholm through its stunning archipelago. It's a terrifying task for the skipper, for if he gets it wrong he could cause untold damage to a nearby historic windmill or even run aground. Injury strikes the entertainment team, leaving dancer Dulcie paired with a new recruit who doesn't know the routines, and a safety helmet causes problems for excursions agent Timothy and his perfectly styled hair. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The season of goodwill descends on the Larkin household, and the family celebrates the 'perfick' Christmas. Drama, based on the characters created by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Henry Miller comes to stay with the family, and stirs up tension among Louisa and her children with his strange behaviour and the controversial content of his books. Sven is arrested after the government announces a crackdown on what it considers 'immoral behaviour', and Larry rushes to his aid, acquiring books on fascism and miscarriages of justice from the countess to help him take matters into his own hands. Spiros continues to pine for his wife, and Basil makes a surprise appearance on the island, leaving the family on edge about Aunt Hermione's estate

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is shocked when farmer Simon Langley-Smythe shoots her aunt's dog Snowy while out on a walk, and PC Younger fails to chase down children who have released livestock from the fields. Meanwhile, Carol is concerned when she sees Julie Langley-Smythe nursing a black eye

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic is furious with Chris Tate, Kelly tells Mandy that she still intends to have an abortion, and Sam turns on Zak for never teaching him to read

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah storms to Rachel's house and wants to see Jack. The Glovers talk to Jan's psychiatrist. Ned is apprehensive about the suggestion that Jan can return home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma struggles to free herself in the water, Tricia assures Vera she will still be able to see Brad, and the Platts and Mike are summoned to the hospital

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is terrified when Don kidnaps her. Vera gives Tricia a hard time about moving out. Chris hopes that Angie will sleep with him. Jim tells Andy not to throw his life away

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: An influx of holiday-makers to Portwenn annoys Martin, and his efforts to point out the dangers of sunbathing to a scantily clad woman prove unpopular. He is also the target of a practical joke in the pub after his fear of blood becomes common knowledge, and grows closer to Louisa when one of her pupils has a serious accident that lands him in hospital. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: It's turnaround day on the Regal Princess, when the crew has to help 3,300 passengers check out with 10,000 pieces of luggage and then welcome the new batch - with just a few hours to clean and prepare 1,800 rooms. As the staff members work against the clock, hotel manager Dirk makes his usual inspections - and will not tolerate any slip in standards. Meanwhile, dancer Dulcie is feeling homesick, and front-desk supervisor Herbert is under pressure to locate some missing luggage and have it delivered at the next port

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The staff compete to win the coveted title of employee of the month, which carries a top prize of $500, as well as deck privileges, tours and extra time off. Deck hand Rhymond is among those with their eye on the top prize, having braved the elements since 5am to keep the ship looking pristine. Meanwhile, Timothy feels sad about leaving his friends on the front desk to start his new job with the shore excursions team, and engineer Scott is left in charge when his boss goes on leave, with jobs including making drinking water from the sea

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy calls on Mavis to offer her condolences. Maxine asks Ashley if he wants to start afresh with her. Mike is thrilled when the insurance company pay out in full

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly discovers Chris has made an appointment for her at a private clinic. Paddy is incensed when he reads the letter from Kim Tate evicting Outhwaite

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel tackles Chris about his relationship with Kelly, Dee and Eric row in the tearooms, and Mandy cooks a meal for Paddy, but it is not what he was expecting

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paul Nicholas guest stars as a Swedish sailor who waits for his ship home in Bernie's funeral parlour - but may not be telling the truth about the contents of the coffin he's escorting. A trio of young joyriders steal Carol's car while she is attending to a patient and decide to experiment with the drugs they find in her medical bag as well as selling them on to interested parties. Rosie and Fergus struggle to get their fledgling relationship off the ground as a series of interruptions plague their dates, while Bellamy starts to wonder whether trying again with Gina is really such a good idea. With Lisa Kay, Vanessa Hehir, Mark Jordon and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is worried about an intruder, and wonders whether to scare the man off or call the police

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid informs Jean he has been given a temporary promotion at work, but she soon begins to doubt what he says. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: The team turns out for another Saturday game. Comedy, starring Josie Lawrence, Timothy Spall, Robert Daws and Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. A seat becomes vacant on the local council when Sam Popham is taken ill, so Pop stands in the election, and Mariette returns from holiday with Charley. David Jason stars

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Spiros spends time with a glamorous Italian family who have recently arrived on the island. Louisa is jealous of the attention he is paying their new neighbours and invites them over to learn more about them. She ends up convinced they are hiding something, while Margo feels threatened by their eldest daughter. Larry joins the fire service, and Gerry falls in love

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a guest at the Aidensfield Arms explains she's searching for her missing son, Blaketon offers to help - but as his hunt progresses, it becomes clear somebody is trying to do him harm.
